requirement.original: Item D: dev 子代理自报修改文件不可信。根本问题：dev 子代理完成工作后，自己填写 dev-report.files_modified 列表。目前没有强制它从 git diff 派生，所以它可以填任何内容，QA 和 changelog-analyst 无法验证真伪。修复范围：(1) commands/dev.md — dev dispatch payload 加入 baseline_head_sha（orchestrator 在 dispatch 前 run git rev-parse HEAD）和 baseline_dirty_snapshot（git status --porcelain=v1）。(2) agents/dev.md — dev agent 完成时，files_modified 必须从 git diff $baseline_head_sha..HEAD --name-only 派生（加上新增 untracked 文件），不能自填；新增 observed_preexisting 字段独立报告在 baseline_dirty_snapshot 中已存在的脏文件。(3) agents/changelog-analyst.md — BULK=false 时，对 dev-report.files_modified 中每个文件检查它是否在 git diff baseline_head_sha..HEAD 里；若不在 diff 里但在 files_modified 里，打印 WARNING 并跳过该文件（warn-and-skip，不拒绝整个提交）。(4) agents/qa.md — QA 验证时，检查 dev-report.files_modified 与 git diff baseline..HEAD 的一致性；不一致时作为 QA 非通过项报告。
requirement.clarified: Items 1, 2, and 4 are already implemented. The genuine gap is item 3: agents/changelog-analyst.md provenance filter classifies provenance_anomaly files but then stages them anyway in BULK=false (line 173: 'Stage them if they appear in the candidate set'). The fix: in BULK=false, exclude provenance_anomaly files from staging with a WARNING. Commit proceeds for remaining files.
requirement.what: Change agents/changelog-analyst.md provenance filter (lines 171-175): in BULK=false mode, files in dev.files_modified absent from git diff <baseline_head_sha> AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot must be removed from the staging candidate set (warn-and-skip), not staged.
requirement.why: A dev subagent can self-report phantom files in files_modified. Currently changelog-analyst stages such anomalous files anyway (they pass through the whitelist). Warn-and-skip closes this escape hatch so only diff-verified files are committed.

included: agents/changelog-analyst.md provenance filter clause (lines 171-175) — BULK=false warn-and-skip behavior
excluded: BULK=true path in changelog-analyst (correct as-is: staging authority is git status, not dev-report)

success_criteria: In BULK=false mode, a file in dev.files_modified that is absent from git diff <baseline_head_sha> AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot is NOT staged and a WARNING is printed for it
The commit proceeds for all other eligible files (not aborted)
In BULK=true mode, behavior is unchanged (provenance filter is enrichment-only)
constraints: Do not touch BULK=true staging logic
Do not change any other agent files
WARNING message must be distinct from the BULK=true enrichment-only warning to avoid confusion

root_cause_analysis.symptom: changelog-analyst stages files claimed by dev.files_modified even when those files are provenance_anomaly (absent from git diff baseline)
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: agents/changelog-analyst.md line 173 explicitly says 'Stage them if they appear in the candidate set' for BULK=false, even after classifying them as provenance_anomaly. The warn-only approach for BULK=false was not the intended design according to the user requirement.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — design gap, not a regression
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The provenance filter was initially designed to prevent anomalous paths from influencing commit message enrichment. The stronger property (don't stage them either) was not carried through for BULK=false.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: A dev subagent can self-report phantom files in dev.files_modified. If those files happen to exist in the working tree (from a prior session), they pass the whitelist check and get staged under BULK=false, making the staging set untrustworthy.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Present in the initial provenance filter implementation; not a regression.
observations: agents/changelog-analyst.md line 173 (inferred): 'Stage them if they appear in the candidate set (for BULK=true, staging authority comes from git status; for BULK=false, staging authority comes from the whitelist), but do not use their paths to determine commit type or scope.' — anomalous BULK=false files are explicitly staged.
agents/changelog-analyst.md lines 171-172 (inferred): provenance_anomaly classification is correct but consequence (line 173) is warn-only for enrichment, not warn-and-skip for staging.
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — no runtime observation of actual staging of a phantom file; the code path is unambiguous from reading)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Direct reading of agents/changelog-analyst.md lines 171-175 shows the staging permission for BULK=false provenance_anomaly files

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not a regression — no bisect needed

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Git-diff derivation MANDATORY section already implements item 2 of the user requirement: files_modified derived from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>, observed_preexisting[] defined. No change needed.
Pre-dispatch baseline capture already implements item 1 of the user requirement: baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot captured and passed in dispatch payload. No change needed.

Provenance check (Step 5) already implements item 4 of the user requirement: dev_provenance_violation and files_modified_underreport_violation are critical FAIL findings when files_modified mismatches git diff. No change needed.

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: BULK=false 时，对 dev-report.files_modified 中每个文件检查它是否在 git diff baseline_head_sha..HEAD 里
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: In BULK=false, every file in dev.files_modified is checked against git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> output before staging

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: 若不在 diff 里但在 files_modified 里，打印 WARNING 并跳过该文件（warn-and-skip，不拒绝整个提交）
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Files absent from diff are excluded from staging with a WARNING; the commit proceeds for remaining eligible files

component_chain.role: Provenance filter — classifies dev.files_modified entries against git diff; currently warns but stages anomalies in BULK=false

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: The `baseline_head_sha` diff is used ONLY as a provenance sanity check for already-whitelisted files. It is NEVER an independent inclusion source
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Prevents provenance diff from adding files NOT in the whitelist to the candidate set

evidence.observed: dev 子代理完成工作后，自己填写 dev-report.files_modified 列表。目前没有强制它从 git diff 派生，所以它可以填任何内容，QA 和 changelog-analyst 无法验证真伪

measured.value: agents/changelog-analyst.md line 173: 'Stage them if they appear in the candidate set (for BULK=true, staging authority comes from git status; for BULK=false, staging authority comes from the whitelist), but do not use their paths to determine commit type or scope.'

expected.value: In BULK=false, provenance_anomaly files from dev.files_modified must be excluded from staging (warn-and-skip), not staged
expected.source: User requirement item 3: 打印 WARNING 并跳过该文件（warn-and-skip，不拒绝整个提交）

evidence.gap: BULK=false provenance_anomaly files are staged (warn-but-stage) instead of excluded (warn-and-skip)

reference_source.description: Direct reading of agents/changelog-analyst.md lines 171-175 confirms the current warn-but-stage behavior for BULK=false provenance_anomaly files

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempts; first analysis of this specific gap

context.recent_commits: a57a7c3 chore(agents+hooks): update changelog-analyst agent and pretool-bash-safety hook; 72e6da4 agents and commands updates

file_contents.agents/changelog-analyst.md:171-175:
- For every path in `dev.files_modified` that is absent from the `git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>` output AND absent from `baseline_dirty_snapshot`, classify it as `provenance_anomaly`.
   - For every path in `dev.files_created`, check via `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard`. If the path is absent from that output AND absent from `baseline_dirty_snapshot`, classify it as `provenance_anomaly`.
4. **Exclude** `provenance_anomaly` paths from commit-message type/scope/summary enrichment derivation. Stage them if they appear in the candidate set ..., but do not use their paths to determine commit type or scope.
5. Log each anomaly with the appropriate source: ...

development_approach.strategy: Edit agents/changelog-analyst.md provenance filter step 4 to split behavior by BULK mode: BULK=false removes provenance_anomaly paths from candidate set + prints WARNING; BULK=true keeps current enrichment-only behavior.

changes.what: Split BULK=false and BULK=true behavior for provenance_anomaly files
changes.before_description: Step 4 says 'Stage them if they appear in the candidate set' for both BULK modes, only excluding from enrichment
changes.after_description: Step 4 says: BULK=false — remove from candidate set and print WARNING 'excluded from staging (BULK=false warn-and-skip)'; BULK=true — stage as before (enrichment-only, staging authority is git status)
changes.rationale: Closes escape hatch where phantom files in dev.files_modified pass through the whitelist and get staged in BULK=false mode

development_approach.validation_approach: Read the updated agents/changelog-analyst.md and verify lines 171-177 contain the BULK=false warn-and-skip clause, the BULK=true enrichment-only clause, and that the pre-existing guard (line 177) is unchanged.

acceptance_criteria.given: BULK=false AND a file in dev.files_modified is absent from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot
acceptance_criteria.then: The file is NOT staged; a WARNING is printed containing 'provenance_anomaly' and 'excluded from staging'; the commit proceeds for remaining eligible files

acceptance_criteria.given: BULK=true AND a file in dev.files_modified is absent from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>
acceptance_criteria.then: Behavior is unchanged from current: the file may still be staged (staging authority is git status); it is excluded from commit-message enrichment only
The text explicitly describes different behavior for BULK=false (warn-and-skip from candidate set) vs BULK=true (enrichment-only)

research_findings.reason: Straightforward, single-file behavior change with clear root cause confirmed by direct code reading
